title: A megacity redraws its relationship with the Nile
dek: New public spaces are reconnecting Cairo residents with the river at the heart of their city.
author: Mariam El-Sayed
body: For generations, long stretches of Cairo’s riverfront have been difficult to reach. A patchwork of new projects is beginning to open passages back to the Nile.
Planners are combining shaded walkways, ferry stops and small cultural spaces. The most promising designs focus on everyday access.
Civic groups are pressing for the riverfront to remain public, affordable and connected to neighbourhoods beyond the city centre.
